Document:
# Smithing
The art of metalworking and weaponcrafting. As a blacksmith, you craft enhanced weapons and armor, and specialize in modular component assembly to customize equipment with stat bonuses and special properties.
## The Blacksmith's Craft
Blacksmiths work at [bold]Anvil workbenches[/bold] to forge weapons, armor, and modular components. Each recipe takes time to complete — watch the progress bar and ensure you don't move or take damage during the process.
The blacksmith's primary resources are: - [bold]Iron Bars[/bold] — basic metal building block (1 bar crafts 1 unit of metal) - [bold]Copper Bars[/bold] — secondary metal for alloys and components - [bold]Wooden Planks[/bold] — handles, hilts, and reinforcement - [bold]Leather[/bold] — wrappings, padding, and flexible components
## Modular Crafting System
### What is Modular Crafting?
Modular crafting allows you to customize weapons and armor by attaching specialized parts. Each weapon and armor piece has [bold]2 attachment slots[/bold] that can accept different component types.
When you attach a modular part to a weapon or armor: 1. The item immediately gains stat bonuses (if the part has them) 2. The item's damage, protection, or speed properties are modified 3. You can attach multiple parts to maximize customization
### Available Modular Parts
#### Stat Crystals (All Slots) Essence shards crafted from basic materials. Each adds +1 to a specific stat when equipped.
- [bold]Strength Essence Shard:[/bold] +1 STR — Crafted from 2 Iron Bars + 1 Copper Bar - [bold]Vitality Essence Shard:[/bold] +1 VIT — Crafted from 2 Iron Bars + 1 Copper Bar - [bold]Dexterity Essence Shard:[/bold] +1 DEX — Crafted from 2 Iron Bars + 1 Copper Bar - [bold]Intelligence Essence Shard:[/bold] +1 INT — Crafted from 2 Iron Bars + 1 Copper Bar
#### Weapon Parts (Blade/Hilt Slots)
- [bold]Damage Blade:[/bold] Increases weapon damage by ~2 Slash/Pierce, ~1 Blunt - Cost: 4 Iron Bars + 2 Copper Bars - Slot: Blade
- [bold]Speed Hilt:[/bold] Increases attack speed by 15% - Cost: 3 Iron Bars + 2 Wooden Planks - Slot: Hilt
#### Armor Parts (Plate/Fastening Slots)
- [bold]Heavy Armor Plate:[/bold] Reduces all damage by ~10-15% (Blunt 85%, Slash 90%, Pierce 80%) - Cost: 5 Iron Bars + 2 Copper Bars - Slot: Armor Plate
- [bold]Protective Padding:[/bold] Reduces all damage by ~8% - Cost: 3 Cloth + 1 Copper Bar - Slot: Fastening
#### Mobility Parts (Fastening Slot)
- [bold]Light Embrace:[/bold] Reduces movement penalty by 5% (walk/sprint +5% speed) - Cost: 2 Iron Bars + 3 Leather - Slot: Fastening
- [bold]Agile Weave:[/bold] Reduces movement penalty by 8-10% (walk +8%, sprint +10% speed) - Cost: 4 Leather - Slot: Fastening
#### Magical Parts (Blade/Plate Slots)
- [bold]Mana Conduit:[/bold] Reduces spell casting cost by 10% - Cost: 3 Copper Bars + 2 Crystal Ore - Slot: Blade or Armor Plate
- [bold]Mana Amplifier:[/bold] Reduces spell casting cost by 15% (requires more crystals) - Cost: 2 Iron Bars + 4 Crystal Ore - Slot: Blade or Armor Plate
## How to Craft Modular Parts
All modular parts are crafted at an [bold]Anvil workbench[/bold].
1. [bold]Gather raw materials:[/bold] - Find ore deposits in the world (Iron Ore, Copper Ore, Gold Ore, or Mithril Ore) - Mine the ore and bring it to the Anvil - Craft metal bars: Each bar requires 10 units of ore - Example: 10 Iron Ore → 1 Iron Bar (use the "Smelt" recipes)
2. [bold]Access the Anvil workbench:[/bold] - Approach the Anvil and interact with it - View available recipes in the crafting menu
3. [bold]Craft a modular part:[/bold] - Find the recipe you want (e.g., "Craft Strength Crystal") - Place the required materials (2 Iron Bars + 1 Copper Bar for stat crystals) - Select the recipe and wait for the progress bar to complete - The finished part will be placed nearby
## How to Attach Modular Parts
1. [bold]Craft or obtain a base weapon or armor[/bold] 2. [bold]Have a modular part in your inventory[/bold] 3. [bold]Examine the weapon or armor[/bold] (right-click or use examine key) 4. [bold]Select the "Attach Part" action[/bold] from the examine menu - A progress bar will appear (1.5-3 seconds depending on part) - Do not move or take damage during assembly - When complete, the part attaches and applies its bonuses
Once attached, the weapon or armor [bold]permanently keeps the stat bonuses[/bold] and mechanical benefits of that part. You can attach multiple parts to the same item (up to the number of available slots).
## Disassembling Modular Items
If you damage a modular weapon or armor enough (take ~50 damage), it will break and drop all attached parts as separate items. You can then collect the parts and reuse them on other equipment.
## Advanced Smithing Tips
- [bold]Combine stat crystals with weapon parts:[/bold] Attach a Strength Shard + Damage Blade to your sword for both +1 STR and +2 damage - [bold]Layer armor protection:[/bold] Use Heavy Plate + Protective Padding on your cuirass to stack damage reduction - [bold]Balance mobility:[/bold] Heavy armor penalizes movement; offset this with Light Embrace or Agile Weave parts - [bold]Specialize for magic:[/bold] If you also cast spells, add Mana Conduit to your staff for cheaper casting - [bold]Collect and reuse:[/bold] Modular parts are valuable — keep extras in storage to swap between different weapons and builds
## Related Systems
- [textlink="Crafting" link="OE14Crafting"] — overview of all crafting methods - [textlink="Combat" link="OE14Combat"] — how weapon stats affect damage - [textlink="Jobs" link="OE14_EN_Jobs"] — the Blacksmith job details and requirements
